hub_subtitle: From hesitant communicator to confident global business leader

lesson.menu_title: Handling Problems After Delivery — Reporting & Saving the Relationship
lesson.menu_desc: When something goes wrong after the goods arrive, the relationship is on the line. Acknowledge the issue, reassure the client, escalate it to the right person, sort out a discrepancy, and protect the goodwill you built — then relay it all to your team with reported speech (she said the box was damaged, he told me he would reimburse us) — 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 15 — Handling Problems After Delivery
lesson.title_tag: Professor View — Milton Sayegh | Aula 15 — Handling Problems After Delivery | Business English for Luxury Export | Alumni by Better

listenings.text: Hi Milton, it is Karen from Halstead, and I am afraid I am calling with a problem. The shipment arrived this morning, but two of the necklace boxes were damaged in transit, and one bracelet is missing from the order entirely. I checked the paperwork twice, so there is a real discrepancy between what we ordered and what reached us. I am not upset with you, but the store opening is in twelve days, and right now I am short on stock. I would really like to know what you can do, and how quickly. Please call me back today if you can. I want to sort this out before it becomes a bigger issue. Thank you, Milton.

Hey team, Milton here. We have a situation with the Halstead order, so listen closely. Karen told me that two boxes were damaged in transit and that one bracelet never arrived, and she said there was a discrepancy in the paperwork. Here is how we handle it. First, I already called her back and acknowledged the problem honestly, because hiding it would cost us the relationship. I promised her I would reimburse the damaged pieces and ship a replacement bracelet by express, and I told her we would cover that cost as a goodwill gesture. I want this escalated to me directly, not buried in a queue. Reassure her at every step, keep her updated daily, and let us turn an outstanding problem into the reason she trusts us for years. A problem handled well is worth more than a delivery that just goes smoothly. Thanks, everyone.
